attributes.content:
## Overview

The Canvas card gives the workspace a structured home for infinite canvas for freeform arrangement of cards, images, drawing, and connections.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Canvas usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[AI Image]]** -- Canvas can incorporate generated visuals or treat image output as a linked asset.
- **[[Prompt Template]]** -- Prompt-driven drafting or summarization can turn Canvas into a reusable AI-assisted workflow.
- **[[Project]]** -- Canvas can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Task]]** -- Canvas often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Document]]** -- Canvas can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Brand Guide]]** -- Brand Guide can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Canvas.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Canvas as the structured home for infinite canvas for freeform arrangement of cards, images, drawing, and connections and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Canvas when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Canvas stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Canvas
cardInfo.summary: Infinite canvas for freeform arrangement of cards, images, drawing, and connections.
